req.title: Wire transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct decision zones: (1) normal transfers below $100,000 with valid 2FA — positive path; (2) transfers with stale 2FA — negative/authentication path; (3) transfers at the exact $100,000 threshold — boundary where the system must decide between auto-queue and manual review. BVA demands testing at $99,999.99 (below threshold), $100,000.00 (exact threshold), and $100,000.01 (above threshold). EP partitions the input space into [0, 100000) and [100000, ∞). STC covers the 2FA state machine: fresh→authorized vs stale→401. DT captures the cross-product of 2FA-state × amount-range. NEG covers the stale-2FA rejection. The violation in test[5] was heuristic=STC on a negative kind test; negative tests for invalid input must use NEG. Fixed by changing that test's heuristic to NEG. Together these give full coverage of stated error pathways and boundary behaviour.

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_below_threshold_with_valid_2fa
preconditions: Customer account exists and is funded
2FA verified within the last 5 minutes (fresh session token available)
Transfer amount $50,000.00 is within the auto-approve equivalence class [0, 100000)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=50000.00, currency=USD, and valid 2FA session header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 status code indicating wire queued
Response body contains status=queued
Response body contains compliance_check_started=true
No manual_review flag present or manual_review=false
trace: R-FIN-031: wire queued on valid submission
R-FIN-031: amounts below $100,000 do not require manual review
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_below_threshold_with_valid_2fa(fresh_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "50000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert body["compliance_check_started"] is True
    assert body.get("manual_review", False) is False

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_rejected_when_2fa_stale
preconditions: 2FA token is older than 5 minutes (stale session)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=50000.00 and a stale 2FA session header (timestamp > 5 min ago)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 401 status code
Response body contains error code indicating stale or expired 2FA
No transfer record created in the system
trace: R-FIN-031: precondition — 2FA verified in last 5 min; violation triggers error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_rejected_when_2fa_stale(stale_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "50000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=stale_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "authentication" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    transfer_id = body.get("transfer_id")
    assert transfer_id is None

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_at_exact_threshold_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists and is funded with at least $100,000.00
2FA verified within the last 5 minutes (fresh session token available)
Transfer amount is exactly $100,000.00 — the defined threshold boundary
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=100000.00, currency=USD, and valid 2FA session header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 202 status code indicating accepted but pending manual review
Response body contains status=pending_review or equivalent
Response body contains manual_review=true
Response body contains compliance_check_started=true
trace: R-FIN-031: transfers above $100,000 require manual review — boundary at exactly $100,000 triggers this path
R-FIN-031: 202 returned when manual review required
R-FIN-031: compliance check started regardless of manual review flag
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_at_exact_threshold_requires_manual_review(fresh_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "100000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    assert body.get("compliance_check_started") is True
    assert body.get("status") in ("pending_review", "manual_review_required")

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_one_cent_below_threshold_not_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists and is funded
2FA verified within the last 5 minutes (fresh session token available)
Transfer amount is $99,999.99 — one cent below the $100,000 threshold (lower boundary neighbour)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=99999.99, currency=USD, and valid 2FA session header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 status code indicating wire queued without manual review
Response body contains status=queued
Response body manual_review is absent or false
Response body contains compliance_check_started=true
trace: R-FIN-031: transfers below $100,000 do not require manual review; $99,999.99 is the last valid auto-approve value
R-FIN-031: BVA lower neighbour of the manual-review threshold
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_one_cent_below_threshold_not_manual_review(fresh_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "99999.99",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert body.get("manual_review", False) is False
    assert body.get("compliance_check_started") is True

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_valid_2fa_triggers_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account exists and is funded with at least $200,000.00
2FA verified within the last 5 minutes (fresh session token available)
Transfer amount $150,000.00 falls in the manual-review partition [100000, ∞)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=150000.00, currency=USD, and valid 2FA session header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 202 status code
Response body contains manual_review=true
Response body contains compliance_check_started=true
Response body contains a transfer_id for tracking
trace: R-FIN-031: DT rule — valid 2FA AND amount >= 100000 => 202 + manual_review=true
R-FIN-031: compliance check started for all accepted transfers
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_valid_2fa_triggers_manual_review(fresh_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "150000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True
    assert body.get("compliance_check_started") is True
    assert body.get("transfer_id") is not None

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_stale_2fa_rejected
preconditions: Customer account exists and is funded with at least $200,000.00
2FA token is older than 5 minutes (stale session)
Transfer amount $150,000.00 would normally require manual review
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=150000.00, currency=USD, and a stale 2FA session header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 401 status code — 2FA staleness takes precedence over amount threshold
Response body contains error indicating stale or expired 2FA
No transfer record or transfer_id returned
trace: R-FIN-031: 401 when 2FA stale — authentication failure supersedes amount-based routing
R-FIN-031: NEG — invalid 2FA state combined with above-threshold amount still yields 401
R-FIN-031: DT cross-product: stale 2FA × any amount => reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"

def test_wire_transfer_above_threshold_with_stale_2fa_rejected(stale_2fa_headers, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account": funded_account["id"],
        "destination": "DEST-BANK-001",
        "amount": "150000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=stale_2fa_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "authentication" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert body.get("transfer_id") is None
